rationale: The core-0.34 candidate is expected to fail EXACTLY the class-(c) golden/version assertions (the taxonomy §4.2 inventory) and NOTHING else. compare-spike-runs.js --mode classify reads the candidate's `npm test` and `test:browser` vitest --reporter=json output and asserts the observed failing (file -> count) set equals `node`/`browser` below EXACTLY: any ADDITIONAL failing file, any file whose failure COUNT differs, or any expected-red file that fully PASSED (an engine flip — re-triage) fails the candidate arm. This is machine-enforced, not narrated: a bare '11 failures' is insufficient. See docs/engine-assertion-taxonomy-2026-07.md §4.2 and docs/rapier-034-spike-2026-07.md §4.

node.reporter: npm test (vitest run --reporter=json), Node, deterministic + ordinary flavors, tests/browser/** excluded

tests/evaluation-determinism.test.js.asserts: rapierVersion staleness pin (0.19.3 -> 0.19.3-c13133ad.0) + eval-A/B/C/D golden trace digests

tests/population-determinism.test.js.asserts: rapierVersion/dt pin, fitness-vector digest (bded0d30 -> measured), champion solo-trace digest

tests/bench-schema.test.js.asserts: report.meta.rapier == package.json dependency pin { compat: '0.19.3', deterministicCompat: '0.19.3' }

browser.reporter: npm run test:browser (vitest run --config vitest.browser.config.js --reporter=json), pinned Chromium
browser.note: The local historical spike SKIPPED test:browser, so per-file browser counts are FIRST MEASURED by the heavy CI dispatch (C5); tighten this section from that run's observed failing set. The two browser determinism files are expected to fail on the same class-(c) golden digests that moved on Node — and the load-bearing invariant is that the candidate's MEASURED browser digest equals its MEASURED Node digest (Node<->Chromium agreement), asserted separately by --mode classify.

tests/browser/evaluation-determinism.tes.asserts: A-D golden trace digests + rapierVersion, in Chromium

tests/browser/population-determinism.tes.asserts: population fitness-vector + champion-trace digests + rapierVersion, in Chromium

mustPassInvariants.dtReadback: world.timestep reads back Math.fround(1/60) = 0.01666666753590107 on the candidate (the probe/runner dt tooth stays GREEN — else the whole physics suite cascade-reds for the wrong reason)
mustPassInvariants.internalDeterminismGateA: the determinism gate's 'two fresh worlds agree on digest + every checkpoint' (internal repeatability) PASSES on the candidate for A-D; only gate (d) 'matches committed lock' is allowed to move
mustPassInvariants.nodeChromiumAgreement: the candidate's MEASURED digests are identical between the Node and Chromium determinism-gate failure printouts (cross-env determinism holds on the new engine)

classBFlipWatch.probe:timing profiler re-enable DRIFT: the single class-(b) engine-finding the local run saw (exit 1 on probe:timing, adoption-critical sub-checks green); recorded, not a contract
